content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • • Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660          322-5660          322-5901          322-5441          322-6644

April 2, 1980

Steven A. Herum
Neumiller & Beardslee
P.O. Drawer 20
Stockton, California 95201

Dear Steve:

This letter will confirm the advice you received in our
telephone conversation of March 26, 1980. Individual attorneys
in a law firm do not accumulate their time spent on activities
to influence legislative or administration action to determine
whether the reporting requirements of a lobbyist have been acti-
vated with respect to the law firm. The activities of each
individual attorney determine whether or not that attorney has
satisfied either the time or compensation tests described in
2 Cal. Admin. Code §18239(e).

If an attorney attempts to influence legislative or adminis-
trative action as the agent of another attorney the time and
compensation of the agent are attributable to the principal
attorney. "Agent" includes only those persons who act under the
direct supervision or direct orders of another person to accom-
plish the specific goals of that person. 2 Cal. Admin. Code
§18239(c).

Please contact me if I can be of any further assistance

Sincerely,

J. Richard Eichman
Accounting Specialist

JRE:bw

ROBERT L. BEARDSLEE
THOMAS J. SHEPHARD
DUNCAN R. MCPHERSON
RUDY G. BILAWSKI
ROBERT C. MORRISON
WILLIAM E. DAVIS
ARTHUR COFOD
JAMES A. ASKEW
JOHN R. BYRD
JOHN W. STOVALL
NELSON E. BAHLER
KENNETH H. LEVY

BENJAMIN F. GREEN
DONALD W. WEST
JAMES S. ECKLES
FRANK E. LAUSIN
TIMOTHY H. B. YARYAN
ROBERT J. CARTER
RALPH C. OGDEN III
MARC M. GREENBERG
STEVEN A. HERUM
CARTER P. HOLLY
LESLEY D. HOLLAND

Neumiller & Beardslee
ATTORNEYS AND COUNSELORS

400 FIRST FEDERAL PLAZA BUILDING
6 SOUTH EL DORADO STREET
P. O. DRAWER 20
STOCKTON, CALIFORNIA 95201
(209) 948-8200

CHARLES L. NEUMILLER
1873-1933

GEORGE A. DITZ
1889-1971

IRVING L. NEUMILLER
1899-1970

2936-4723

March 26, 1980

MR. J. RICHARD EICHMAN
Accounting Specialist
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Mr. Eichman:

     I want to thank you for your gracious assistance
in connection with my questions concerning lobbying and
periodic reporting. Your guidance in this matter was
helpful and appreciated.

     This letter is merely to confirm our conversation
that individual attorneys in a law firm do not accumulate
their time spent influencing legislative or administrative
action, pursuant to 2 California Administrative Code Section
18239(e)(1) and (2), to determine whether the reporting
requirements of a lobbyist have been activated with respect
to a law firm. From our conversation, I understand the Act
to focus upon the activities of an individual attorney to
determine whether or not that attorney has satisfied either
the compensation test or the time test. The Act does not
contemplate that a law firm would aggregate the time spent or
entitled compensation of individual attorneys in order to
determine whether or not the law firm satisfies the threshold
requirements of 2 Cal. Ad. Code § 18239(e)(1) or (2).

     If it would not be inconvenient, I would appreciate
a short written opinion in this regard. Again, thank you
for your assistance in this matter.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
STEVEN A. HERUM
for
NEUMILLER & BEARDSLEE

SAH:jlg

sections.question: Do individual attorneys in a law firm accumulate their time spent influencing legislative or administrative action to determine whether the reporting requirements of a lobbyist have been activated with respect to a law firm, pursuant to 2 California Administrative Code Section 18239(e)(1) and (2)?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether individual attorneys' time spent on legislative or administrative influence activities should be aggregated at the law firm level to determine if lobbyist reporting requirements are triggered under California Administrative Code Section 18239(e).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a law firm attorney to the FPPC, not an FPPC response letter. The attorney references a prior helpful conversation and requests written confirmation of the guidance received. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present; the question is embedded in the body text. Document dated March 26, 1980.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Do individual attorneys in a law firm accumulate their time spent influencing legislative or administrative action to determine whether the reporting requirements of a lobbyist have been activated with respect to a law firm, pursuant to 2 California Administrative Code Section 18239(e)(1) and (2)?

embedding.summary: A law firm attorney requests written confirmation from the FPPC regarding their prior telephone conversation about whether attorney time spent on lobbying activities should be aggregated at the firm level or evaluated individually to determine lobbyist registration requirements.
